payload.summary: v1 scaffold passes security review with no critical or high findings on implemented code. Banking hard rules (no secrets in code, no PII/PAN in logs, BigDecimal for money, idempotency on financial POST, audit-via-outbox for state changes, account masking, sealed-error-detail handler) are upheld. The dominant risk theme is the deferred security posture (SecurityConfig stub + AccountClientStub without profile guard + STUB_CUSTOMER_ID in controller). These are acceptable for an isolated v1 scaffold but are NOT shippable to any environment beyond local developer machines. All deferred items are tracked in must_fix_before_staging with explicit gating advice (Spring profile guards + startup fail-fast on missing JWT issuer). Approving handoff to QA so functional + idempotency coverage can be locked in; security hardening (US-006) must land before the service is exposed.

findings.description: JWT resource-server validation is commented out. Filter chain declares anyRequest().authenticated() but http.oauth2ResourceServer(jwt) is not wired, so no JwtDecoder is registered. Behavior in production would be: every request to /api/v1/** is rejected as anonymous (effectively a deny-all). Because @WithMockUser bypasses the filter chain in integration tests, the misconfiguration cannot be detected by the existing test suite. There is no startup guard that fails the boot if profile=prod and no issuer-uri is configured.
findings.exploitability: Low-direct (deny-all in absence of test mocks) but high-systemic: a future commit that adds .permitAll() to any endpoint, or a maintainer who uncomments oauth2ResourceServer(jwt) without supplying issuer-uri, will produce a silently-unauthenticated production deployment. The lack of fail-fast on missing issuer is the real defect.
findings.remediation: Add a @ConditionalOnExpression bean that throws BeanInitializationException when ENVIRONMENT in (staging, prod) and spring.security.oauth2.resourceserver.jwt.issuer-uri is blank. Wire oauth2ResourceServer(jwt) unconditionally and let Spring fail to start without a configured JWKS. Document in README under 'Production deployment checklist'.

findings.description: STUB_CUSTOMER_ID (00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001) is hardcoded and passed as both initiatorUserId and initiatorCustomerId on every request. Combined with the deferred JWT validation in SecurityConfig, this means the CreateTransferUseCase.findById ownership check (line 194) succeeds for every caller as long as transfers were created under the same stub principal. Effectively: any client that can reach the endpoint can read any transfer created by any other client. This is an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) the moment the service is exposed.
findings.exploitability: High IF the service is exposed without SecurityConfig hardening; zero if the service stays on the developer laptop. The risk is therefore deployment-environment-gated, not exploitability-gated. The two flaws (permit-all chain + stub principal) compose into a money-data leak.
findings.remediation: Inject Authentication or @AuthenticationPrincipal Jwt and extract sub claim (UUID) and customer_id claim. Throw 401 if either is absent. Remove the STUB_CUSTOMER_ID constant entirely. Gate the controller with @Profile("!prod") until US-006 lands a real JWT decoder. Add an architecture test (ArchUnit) that fails the build if any controller references a hardcoded UUID literal.

findings.description: AccountClientStub is annotated @Component with no profile guard, no @ConditionalOnProperty, and no @ConditionalOnMissingBean. Spring will auto-wire it in any environment, including staging and prod. The stub always returns ACTIVE with 10,000,000 THB balance, so once SecurityConfig is hardened the saga will execute real transfer state transitions backed by fake account data. This is a money-safety hazard: a transfer would be marked COMPLETED in the transfers table and a TransferCompleted event would be published to Kafka, even though no real debit/credit ever occurred.
findings.exploitability: Critical IF the service ships to any environment that real customers or downstream systems read from; the entire ledger state would be fictional. The bug is silent (no exception, no warning, no metric).
findings.remediation: Add @Profile("!prod & !staging") AND @ConditionalOnProperty(name="transfer.account-client.stub.enabled", havingValue="true", matchIfMissing=false). Log a WARN at startup in an @EventListener(ApplicationReadyEvent.class): 'ACCOUNT CLIENT STUB IS ACTIVE — DO NOT use in production'. Fail the K8s readiness probe if ENVIRONMENT in (staging, prod) and no real AccountClient implementation is present (a @ConditionalOnMissingBean(AccountClient.class) startup guard bean that throws).

findings.description: DB_PASSWORD default value 'transfer_pass' is baked into the YAML via ${DB_PASSWORD:transfer_pass}. If the env var is missing in any environment (including a misconfigured staging/prod), the service silently starts with this known credential. Static credentials baked into version-controlled config are a known-weakness pattern even when local-only intended.
findings.exploitability: Low while service is local-only; medium if a misconfigured K8s manifest omits the secret reference — service boots with the known credential and may connect to a wrong DB or fail open. Credential is already in git history, so 'transfer_pass' must be treated as compromised forever.
findings.remediation: Change to ${DB_PASSWORD} with no default — missing env var causes startup failure. Move local-only defaults to application-local.yml (gitignored or profile-scoped). Add a CI secret-scan gate (gitleaks / trufflehog) to block re-introduction. Add a README note: 'DB_PASSWORD must be supplied by Vault/CSI mount in K8s; never via plain env var.'

findings.description: MDC.put('correlationId', ...) and MDC.put('idempotencyKeyPrefix', ...) are not bounded by a try/finally with MDC.remove. On thread-pool reuse the next, unrelated request handled by the same Tomcat worker will see the previous request's correlationId/idempotencyKeyPrefix until the next put() overwrites them. This produces misleading correlation in logs — a security and audit concern when investigating fraud or PII access.
findings.exploitability: Low (no direct exploit) but log integrity is a regulator-relevant control. False correlation between requests in an audit trail could mislead an investigation or allow log-injection of false context if combined with another flaw.
findings.remediation: Install a ServletFilter that clears MDC at the end of every request (MDC.clear() in finally). Or wrap the controller bodies in try { ... } finally { MDC.clear(); }. Verify by an integration test that runs two requests on the same thread and asserts MDC is empty between them.

findings.description: Outbox row headers field is hardcoded to '{}'. The relay (US-007) will copy the headers JSON into Kafka headers; an empty header means W3C traceparent and correlationId are lost on the producer side. Cross-service correlation breaks at the Kafka boundary — an auditor reconstructing a fraud chain will lose the trace at the event publication point.
findings.exploitability: Not directly exploitable; weakens forensic capability. Material for SOC2 / PCI-DSS Req. 10 (track and monitor all access) and BoT IT-regulation audit-trail requirements.
findings.remediation: Build a small headers map with at least {'correlationId': transfer.getCorrelationId(), 'traceparent': MDC.get('traceparent'), 'eventId': eventId} and serialize via objectMapper.writeValueAsString(...). Zero perf cost. Add a test that asserts headers contain correlationId.

findings.description: log.info('saga.started ... src={}', transfer.getSourceAccountId()) — passes an AccountId instance whose toString() masks to last-4 chars. This is intentional and correct, but is fragile: a future maintainer could change AccountId.toString to return the full UUID without breaking any test, immediately leaking PII into prod logs. Defense-in-depth is missing — there is no explicit log-masking pattern at the call site.
findings.exploitability: Currently safe (mask works). Becomes a PDPA/GDPR finding the moment AccountId.toString is refactored. Adversarial scenario: a developer following Lombok @ToString convention silently regenerates toString in an IDE.
findings.remediation: Either (a) make AccountId.toString final and add a JUnit test in MoneyTest-pattern asserting the masked output, or (b) refactor the log statement to log.info('... src_suffix={}', transfer.getSourceAccountId().toFullString().substring(28)) — explicit masking at the call site. Add a Spotbugs/PMD rule prohibiting toString-of-UUID in log statements.

findings.description: Outbox payload includes full sourceAccountId and destinationAccountId UUIDs (lines 89-91) which will be published to Kafka and copied to all downstream consumers. Account UUIDs are PDPA personal-data-adjacent identifiers (they identify an account, which identifies a customer). The current payload is appropriate for inter-service events, but the persistence + Kafka transport requires explicit data-minimization analysis and a retention policy on the topic. The payload does NOT include memo (good — memo is free-text and could carry PII) and does NOT include balance (good).
findings.exploitability: Not a leak per se — events must contain account IDs to be useful. The concern is governance: every downstream consumer inherits PII processing responsibility, and the transfer_outbox table needs a documented retention/purge schedule.
findings.remediation: Document the event schema as 'personal data — internal only' in OpenAPI/AsyncAPI metadata. Confirm Kafka topic transfer.events ACLs restrict to authorized services only (DevOps task). Add a retention purge job for transfer_outbox once dispatched=true && created_at < now() - 30d (data minimization). Confirm with compliance team that account UUIDs are the minimum necessary identifier (vs e.g. masked refs).

findings.description: Actuator exposes 'prometheus' and 'metrics' endpoints. SecurityConfig permits /actuator/prometheus without auth. In prod, scraping endpoints must be reachable only from the Prometheus pod via NetworkPolicy. There is no in-app guard (e.g., a separate management.server.port on an internal-only listener). Acceptable for v1 but must be paired with K8s NetworkPolicy before staging.
findings.exploitability: Information disclosure (per-endpoint latency, JVM metrics, datasource pool stats). Useful to an attacker for timing-based reconnaissance. Not directly money-impacting.
findings.remediation: Set management.server.port: 9090 and management.server.address: 127.0.0.1 in production profile so actuator binds only to a sidecar-reachable port. Combine with K8s NetworkPolicy restricting port 9090 to Prometheus pod only. Disable swagger-ui in prod (.requestMatchers springdoc paths under @Profile('!prod')).

findings.description: POST /api/v1/transfers has no rate-limiting filter, no per-customer throttle, and no max-payload size guard beyond Spring defaults. The Idempotency-Key store could be flooded by a malicious client posting unique keys to exhaust DB rows (TTL=24h). For a financial endpoint with p95<1s SLA, the lack of bucketing also enables brute-force enumeration of account UUIDs (low signal but possible).
findings.exploitability: DoS-class. Idempotency table growth could exhaust DB storage; account-enumeration is low-yield. Not immediately exploitable in v1 (no exposure), but per OWASP A04 and ASVS V11 rate-limiting is expected.
findings.remediation: Add a Bucket4j or Resilience4j RateLimiter at the controller: e.g., @RateLimiter(name='transferCreate', fallbackMethod=...) with 10 req/sec per JWT subject. Add @PreAuthorize claim-scope check ('hasAuthority(SCOPE_transfer.write)'). Document the rate-limit in OpenAPI rate-limit headers (X-RateLimit-Remaining). Confirm the TTL purge job for transfer_idempotency is scheduled and tested.

findings.description: SHA-256 is used to hash the Idempotency-Key for the lookup key. SHA-256 (without HMAC or salt) is fast and unsalted, so if the transfer_idempotency table were leaked an attacker could brute-force the original keys (UUIDs have ~128 bits entropy so brute force is infeasible, but the construction is not the textbook 'keyed hash' pattern). Acceptable because Idempotency-Key is a client-generated UUID v4, not a secret; the keyHash is only an index, not an authenticator.
findings.exploitability: None. Idempotency-Key is not a credential. Note for the record so a future maintainer does not repurpose this hash for authentication.
findings.remediation: Document at the call site: '// keyHash is an index, NOT an authenticator. Do not use sha256(key) for authn elsewhere.' If the design ever uses Idempotency-Key as a tenant-isolating capability, switch to HMAC-SHA-256 with a per-environment secret (Vault-rotated).

findings.description: ProblemDetail response carries traceId from MDC.get('traceId'), but nothing in the codebase populates MDC.traceId (only correlationId and idempotencyKeyPrefix are set in TransferController). Effectively traceId is always null in error responses — a cross-service correlation handle visible to the client is broken before it ships. Defense-in-depth concern: the missing traceId means an auditor cannot link a customer-visible error to the server-side span.
findings.remediation: Either rename to MDC.get('correlationId') (works immediately), OR add a Micrometer-tracing baggage propagator + W3C traceparent parser ServletFilter that populates MDC.traceId from the inbound traceparent header. Verify by an integration test asserting the response body's traceId equals the inbound traceparent.

scans.secrets: 1 finding (DB_PASSWORD default in application.yml — see finding above)

controls_met: Req-3 (no PAN/CVV/track data anywhere in the service — verified by grep)
Req-4 (no card data in transit; THB transfer payload contains only UUIDs and amount)
Req-6.5.1 (injection — JPA parameterized via Spring Data + @Query named params, no String.format on SQL)
Req-6.5.5 (improper error handling — RFC 7807 ProblemDetail without stack traces in body)
Req-8 (n/a — no user credentials handled in this service)
pci_dss.notes: Service is OUT of PCI-DSS card-data scope. Handles intra-bank transfer references and account UUIDs only. No PAN, no CVV, no track data. Cardholder data flow is the responsibility of a separate card-tokenization service (not in this scope). The service is in scope for general bank IT-security regulations (BoT) and PDPA, not PCI.

gdpr.retention: transfers: 7y (audit/legal); transfer_idempotency: 24h TTL; transfer_outbox: dispatched + 30d (TBD with compliance)

pdpa.consent_management: Out-of-scope for this service — handled by identity-service consent registry (Thailand PDPA Section 19). This service operates under the customer's existing banking contract (PDPA lawful basis: contract performance).
pdpa.data_residency: All data (PostgreSQL, Kafka, logs) MUST reside in Thailand. K8s cluster + DB cluster must be pinned to ap-southeast-1 (Bangkok) or on-prem TH per BoT cross-border data rules. DevOps to enforce via K8s topology constraint.

banking_hard_rules_check.jwt_rs256: deferred (US-006); SecurityConfig is permit-all in v1 — see Finding S-01
banking_hard_rules_check.rate_limit_on_auth: deferred (no auth endpoint in this service; see Finding S-10 for transfer-endpoint rate limit)
banking_hard_rules_check.notes: no_secrets_in_code=false because of DB_PASSWORD default in application.yml (Finding S-04, low severity). All other implemented hard rules pass: Money via BigDecimal NUMERIC(19,4) (Money.java + V001 schema); idempotency via SHA-256 keyHash + 24h TTL + 409 on conflict; outbox row written same-tx as business write (audit-for-state-changes); AccountId.toString masks to last 4 chars; Transfer.toString limits to non-PII fields; saga and use-case logs use masked AccountId or correlation_id only; outbox payload omits memo and balance; mass-assignment safe because TransferRequest is a record with explicit fields validated by @Valid, and entity setters are package-private-accessible only via the adapter (not bound to controller).

POST /api/v1/transfers.spoofing: Mitigation deferred to US-006 (JWT RS256 validation). v1 uses hardcoded STUB_CUSTOMER_ID — must NOT ship beyond local dev. Finding S-02.
POST /api/v1/transfers.tampering: BigDecimal money + NUMERIC(19,4) + @Version optimistic lock + UNIQUE constraint on reference_number. Idempotency checksum (SHA-256 of canonical request payload) detects replay-with-mutation and returns 409. Pass.
POST /api/v1/transfers.repudiation: Outbox row TransferRequested/Completed/Failed written in same tx; correlation_id persisted on every row. PartIal: signed-event payload not yet (acceptable v1 — signing in US-007 relay). State-change audit present via outbox.
POST /api/v1/transfers.information_disclosure: AccountId masked in logs/toString; balances never logged; ProblemDetail responses contain no stack traces; memo not in outbox payload. Pass. Concern: Finding S-05 (MDC leak across requests).
POST /api/v1/transfers.denial_of_service: No rate limit in v1 (Finding S-10). Resilience4j config present for accountClient but no decorator on the controller. Idempotency table growth is a DoS vector (TTL purge job not yet implemented — out-of-scope for v1 code, in DevOps cron).
POST /api/v1/transfers.elevation_of_privilege: @PreAuthorize('isAuthenticated()') is the sole guard. No claim-scope check (e.g. hasAuthority('SCOPE_transfer.write')). Acceptable v1 — must add scope-based authz before US-006.

GET /api/v1/transfers/{transferId}.spoofing: Same as POST — JWT validation deferred.
GET /api/v1/transfers/{transferId}.repudiation: GET logged at INFO with correlation_id. Pass.
GET /api/v1/transfers/{transferId}.information_disclosure: Ownership check at use-case line 194 throws TransferNotFoundException (404) — does not leak resource existence to non-owners (defense in depth vs IDOR). Pass given the JWT subject is correctly extracted (currently it is NOT — see Finding S-02). Pass-with-condition.
GET /api/v1/transfers/{transferId}.denial_of_service: No rate limit. UUID path param prevents enumeration (~2^128 keyspace). Acceptable.
GET /api/v1/transfers/{transferId}.elevation_of_privilege: Same as POST — needs scope/role check before US-006.

v1_scaffold_acceptances: AccountClientStub auto-wired with canned 10M THB ACTIVE response is acceptable ONLY because the service is not deployed anywhere reachable; must NOT ship to any environment beyond local dev (gating required, see must_fix_before_staging item 1).

SecurityConfig with commented-out oauth2ResourceServer JWT validation is acceptable ONLY because no production traffic reaches this service; @WithMockUser bypass in integration tests is acceptable for v1 functional verification (see must_fix item 2).
STUB_CUSTOMER_ID hardcoded in TransferController is acceptable ONLY in combination with the above two gates (see must_fix item 3).
DB_PASSWORD default 'transfer_pass' baked into application.yml is acceptable for local dev convenience (see must_fix item 4).
No rate-limit on POST /api/v1/transfers is acceptable for v1 scaffold; must be added before any traffic reaches the service (see must_fix item 5).
OutboxEventPublisher.headers hardcoded to '{}' is acceptable in v1 because no Kafka relay is wired yet (US-007); must be fixed before the relay ships (see must_fix item 6).
MDC.put without try/finally MDC.clear is acceptable in v1 (single-threaded tests, no thread reuse observed); must be fixed before production load (see must_fix item 7).
ProblemDetail traceId MDC key always null is acceptable in v1 (no real cross-service tracing yet); must be wired with W3C traceparent baggage propagator before staging (see must_fix item 8).
must_fix_before_staging: ITEM-1 [CRITICAL-FOR-DEPLOY]: AccountClientStub must be gated with @Profile('!prod & !staging') + @ConditionalOnProperty('transfer.account-client.stub.enabled') AND a real AccountClient adapter (Feign/WebClient with Resilience4j) must be present. Add a startup-failure bean that throws if ENVIRONMENT in (staging,prod) and no real adapter is found. (Finding S-03)

ITEM-2 [CRITICAL-FOR-DEPLOY]: SecurityConfig.oauth2ResourceServer(jwt) must be uncommented AND spring.security.oauth2.resourceserver.jwt.issuer-uri must be supplied from Vault. Add a startup-failure @ConditionalOnExpression that throws BeanInitializationException if ENVIRONMENT in (staging,prod) and issuer-uri is blank. (Finding S-01)

ITEM-3 [CRITICAL-FOR-DEPLOY]: STUB_CUSTOMER_ID constant must be deleted from TransferController. Replace with @AuthenticationPrincipal Jwt and extract sub (initiatorUserId) + customer_id (initiatorCustomerId) claims. Throw 401 if absent. Must land atomically with ITEM-2. (Finding S-02)

ITEM-4 [BEFORE-PROD]: DB_PASSWORD default 'transfer_pass' must be removed from application.yml. Use ${DB_PASSWORD} with no default so missing env var causes startup failure. Move local-only defaults to application-local.yml. Add CI secret-scan gate. (Finding S-04)

ITEM-5 [BEFORE-PROD]: Add rate-limit on POST /api/v1/transfers (Bucket4j or Resilience4j @RateLimiter at 10 req/sec per JWT subject). Add @PreAuthorize scope check ('hasAuthority(SCOPE_transfer.write)'). (Finding S-10)
ITEM-6 [BEFORE-US-007-RELAY-SHIPS]: OutboxEventPublisher must populate headers with at least {correlationId, traceparent, eventId}. Required for Kafka consumer correlation. (Finding S-06)

ITEM-7 [BEFORE-STAGING]: Add a ServletFilter that calls MDC.clear() in finally at end of every request, OR wrap controller bodies in try/finally. Add integration test asserting MDC empty between requests on same thread. (Finding S-05)

ITEM-8 [BEFORE-STAGING]: Wire Micrometer-tracing + W3C traceparent ServletFilter that populates MDC.traceId from inbound traceparent header. Either that or rename the ProblemDetail traceId key to 'correlationId' for v1. (Finding S-12)

ITEM-9 [BEFORE-STAGING]: Apply DevOps controls — actuator on internal management port 9090, K8s NetworkPolicy restricting Prometheus scrape, swagger-ui disabled in prod profile, K8s topology constraint pinning pods + DB to Thailand region (PDPA data residency). (Finding S-09)
ITEM-10 [BEFORE-PROD]: SCA scan (Dependabot / OWASP-DependencyCheck) must be wired in CI. SBOM published per build. Container scan (Trivy/Grype) on every image build. (DevOps task in S8/S9 phase)

metadata.notes: Approved with 0 critical, 0 high, 3 medium, 7 low, 2 info findings. All medium findings are deployment-environment-gated (stub/scaffold posture) and tracked in must_fix_before_staging with explicit gating advice (Spring profile guards, startup fail-fast on missing JWT issuer, scope-based @PreAuthorize). Implemented code passes all banking hard rules except 'no secrets in code' which is a low-severity YAML default (Finding S-04). PCI-DSS scope = OUT (no card data). PDPA/GDPR pass for the v1 surface area with explicit data-residency and outbox-retention follow-ups. STRIDE table addressed for both endpoints. Coordinated with reviewer artifact (S6) — the dominant theme of stub-posture risks aligns with reviewer's known_limitations_concerns. Handoff to QA so functional/idempotency/security-positive tests can be locked in; harden in US-006 before any environment beyond local dev.
